catalog.description: Generic baseline using public OWASP references. Customize by setting requirements_yaml_url in config.json to point to your organization's requirements.

results.requirement_text: Implement Anti-CSRF protection (e.g. via SameSite cookies) for user sessions. State-changing actions MUST NOT be performed via HTTP GET
results.title: Missing Anti-CSRF Controls
results.finding: no SameSite cookie attribute or CSRF token enforcement; CORS is wide-open at server.ts:182
results.risk: cross-site request forgery against authenticated state-changing endpoints
results.fix: Set SameSite=Strict/Lax on the session cookie and add CSRF tokens, keeping state changes off HTTP GET.

results.requirement_text: Do not store sensitive data in any client-side storage mechanism (localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB, cookies without HttpOnly)
results.title: Session Token In localStorage
results.finding: JWT auth token persisted in localStorage at frontend/src/app/app.guard.ts:18,35
results.risk: any XSS payload can exfiltrate the bearer token; no HttpOnly protection
results.fix: Move the session into an HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookie via a BFF; keep it out of web storage.

results.requirement_text: Configure CORS restrictively; use an explicit allowlist of trusted origins and never use wildcard (*) for authenticated APIs
results.title: Wildcard CORS
results.finding: app.use(cors()) with no origin allowlist at server.ts:182; comment labels it 'Allow everything!'
results.risk: any origin can issue credentialed cross-origin requests to the API
results.fix: Configure an explicit cors({ origin: <allowlist> }); never wildcard for authenticated APIs.

results.requirement_text: Activate Content-Security-Policy (CSP) headers on all internet-facing applications; avoid unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval directives
results.title: No Content Security Policy
results.finding: helmet.contentSecurityPolicy() not configured; no CSP meta in frontend/src/index.html
results.risk: no defense-in-depth against injected script execution
results.fix: Add a restrictive helmet.contentSecurityPolicy({ directives }) without unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval.

results.requirement_text: Set the Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) response header on all internet-facing services with a minimum max-age of one year
results.title: Missing HSTS Header
results.finding: helmet supplies noSniff/frameguard but helmet.hsts() is never applied (server.ts:185-188)
results.risk: no browser HTTPS enforcement; vulnerable to SSL-strip downgrade
results.fix: Add app.use(helmet.hsts({ maxAge: 31536000, includeSubDomains: true })) (min one year).

results.requirement_text: Use only mature, actively maintained frontend frameworks; enable strict TypeScript mode and production build hardening
results.title: Mature Frontend Framework
results.finding: uses Angular (mature, maintained) with TypeScript strict mode

results.requirement_text: Encode all user-controlled output at the frontend to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS); use framework-native binding rather than raw innerHTML
results.title: Unsafe HTML Sink Bypass
results.finding: bypassSecurityTrustHtml() applied to user-controlled values at administration.component.ts:60,78 and track-result.component.ts:48
results.risk: stored/reflected XSS — Angular's sanitizer is explicitly bypassed
results.fix: Remove bypassSecurityTrust* and use framework-native interpolation binding, per the XSS Prevention cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Set Referrer-Policy and Permissions-Policy response headers to limit information leakage and browser feature exposure
results.title: Missing Referrer/Permissions-Policy
results.finding: a feature-policy (payment) header is set (server.ts:189) but no Referrer-Policy and no modern Permissions-Policy
results.risk: referrer leakage and broad browser-feature exposure
results.fix: Add Referrer-Policy and a modern Permissions-Policy header (helmet), per the HTTP Headers cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: On any error (expected or unexpected) the application MUST remain in a secure state and MUST NOT disclose internal details (stack traces, SQL, paths) to clients
results.title: Stack Traces Disclosed On Error
results.finding: Express errorhandler() at server.ts:676 returns full stack traces; errorhandler.title leaks the Express version at server.ts:719
results.risk: internal paths, SQL, and stack frames disclosed to users
results.fix: Restrict errorhandler to development; in production keep a secure state and emit no internal detail, per the Error Handling cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Return generic error messages to clients; log detailed diagnostics server-side only
results.title: Detailed Errors Returned To Clients
results.finding: the dev errorhandler() returns detailed diagnostics in the HTTP response (server.ts:676)
results.risk: diagnostic detail exposed to anonymous clients
results.fix: Return a generic error body to clients and log details server-side only, as the requirement states.

results.requirement_text: Handle authentication and authorization failures consistently to avoid information disclosure through timing or response differences
results.title: Inconsistent Auth Failure Handling
results.finding: login returns a generic credential error, but other endpoints surface differentiated 401/403/500 and error detail across handlers
results.risk: response/timing differences enable user enumeration and probing
results.fix: Normalise authn/authz failure responses (status + body + timing) across handlers, per the Authentication cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Disable technology-disclosure headers (X-Powered-By, Server) or replace them with a non-descriptive value
results.title: Server Header Not Masked
results.finding: x-powered-by is disabled (server.ts:188) but the Express Server header is unmasked and errorhandler.title leaks the Express version (server.ts:719)
results.risk: framework/version fingerprinting aids targeted exploits
results.fix: Also strip/replace the Server header and remove the version from the error title.

results.requirement_text: Sensitive management and debug endpoints MUST NOT be reachable from public networks; serve them on a dedicated internal-only interface or port
results.title: Management Endpoints Public
results.finding: /metrics (Prometheus) served unauthenticated at server.ts:718 and Swagger /api-docs at server.ts:286
results.risk: operational surface and full API schema reachable by anonymous users
results.fix: Move /metrics and /api-docs to an internal-only interface/port, per the REST Security cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Disable all unused features, modules, and default accounts before deploying to production
results.title: Default Accounts / Unused Features Enabled
results.finding: the app seeds default/demo accounts (incl. admin) and ships extensive demo functionality enabled by default
results.risk: known default credentials and surplus features widen the attack surface
results.fix: Remove default/seed accounts and disable unused modules before production, per A05 Security Misconfiguration.

results.requirement_text: Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) in front of all internet-facing applications
results.title: WAF Deployment
results.finding: WAF is a deployment-layer control not present in the repo
results.fix: Deploy a WAF in front of internet-facing services per the requirement.

blueprint.section: 1. Deploy a WAF on Internet-Facing Applications

results.requirement_text: All API-to-API calls MUST use mutual authentication via a standard mechanism (e.g. OAuth 2.0 client credentials, mTLS)
results.title: No Mutual API Authentication
results.finding: service auth relies on a self-signed RS256 JWT whose private key is hardcoded (lib/insecurity.ts:23); no mTLS or client-credentials flow
results.risk: anyone with the embedded key mints valid tokens for any service call
results.fix: Use a standard mutual mechanism (OAuth2 client-credentials or mTLS) per the requirement; stop signing with an in-source key.

results.requirement_text: Apply least-privilege and role-based access control (RBAC) to all endpoints; deny by default
results.title: No RBAC / Deny-By-Default
results.finding: endpoints lack a central deny-by-default authorization layer; access is not role-scoped (pervasive IDOR)
results.risk: vertical/horizontal privilege escalation across users and admin functions
results.fix: Add deny-by-default RBAC middleware and per-endpoint role checks, as the Access Control cheat sheet prescribes.

results.requirement_text: Apply rate limiting to all externally reachable API endpoints to prevent brute-force and scraping attacks
results.title: Rate Limiting Incomplete
results.finding: rateLimit covers /rest/user/reset-password and a few endpoints (server.ts:343,458-471) but not /rest/user/login (server.ts:594) or most APIs
results.risk: credential brute-force and scraping on unthrottled routes
results.fix: Extend express-rate-limit to all externally reachable endpoints, including login.

results.requirement_text: End users MUST be authenticated through a central identity provider using a standard protocol (OIDC/SAML) with mandatory MFA
results.title: No Central IdP / Mandatory MFA
results.finding: custom email/password login in routes/login.ts; no OIDC/SAML IdP, MFA optional
results.risk: credential-based account takeover without federated MFA
results.fix: Authenticate via an OIDC/SAML central IdP with mandatory MFA as the requirement states.

results.requirement_text: Validate OAuth token claims (issuer, audience, expiry) on every request; do not cache authorization decisions beyond the token TTL
results.title: Token Claims Partly Validated
results.finding: expressJwt verifies signature and expiry, but issuer/audience claims are neither set nor validated (lib/insecurity.ts:54,191)
results.risk: a token from any issuer with the (public) key is accepted
results.fix: Set and validate issuer + audience (alongside expiry) on every request, per the JWT cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Enforce resource-level authorization checks to prevent Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR); compare token identity claims against the requested resource
results.title: IDOR / No Resource-Level Authz
results.finding: object access is not ownership-scoped — baskets/orders/feedback resolve by id without comparing the caller's identity claim
results.risk: users read and modify other users' resources by changing the id
results.fix: Compare the JWT identity claim against the requested resource's owner on every object access, per the IDOR cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Validate all external input as restrictively as possible; reject unknown fields rather than ignoring them (allowlist over denylist)
results.title: Input Not Restrictively Validated
results.finding: no schema/allowlist validation layer; raw req.body/req.query flow into SQL, file paths, and parsers
results.risk: injection and traversal across multiple sinks from unvalidated input
results.fix: Add allowlist schema validation per route (express-validator/zod) that rejects unknown fields before sinks.

results.requirement_text: Harden XML parsers to prevent XXE attacks; disable external entity resolution and DTD processing
results.title: XML Parser Allows External Entities
results.finding: libxml.parseXml(data, { noent: true }) at routes/fileUpload.ts:83 resolves external entities (XXE)
results.risk: file disclosure / SSRF via crafted DOCTYPE in uploaded complaint XML
results.fix: Remove noent:true (default false) and disable DTD loading, as the XXE Prevention cheat sheet prescribes.

results.requirement_text: Restrict and validate object graphs during deserialization; never deserialize untrusted data into privileged types
results.title: Unsafe Deserialization Of Uploads
results.finding: yaml.load() runs on attacker-uploaded files in a vm sandbox at routes/fileUpload.ts:117; notevil eval on B2B order data at routes/b2bOrder.ts:9
results.risk: YAML-bomb DoS and sandbox-escape attempts from untrusted content
results.fix: Restrict/validate before deserializing untrusted documents and drop the vm+notevil eval path, per the Deserialization cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Use parameterized queries, prepared statements, or ORM methods for all database access; never concatenate user input into query strings
results.title: Raw String-Interpolated SQL
results.finding: user input concatenated into sequelize.query() at routes/login.ts:34 and routes/search.ts:23
results.risk: authentication bypass and UNION-based data exfiltration via SQL injection
results.fix: Use bound replacements — sequelize.query(sql, { replacements }) — or ORM finders; never concatenate input.

results.requirement_text: Validate, sanitize, and securely store uploaded files; enforce content-type verification, size limits, and safe storage paths
results.title: Insecure Upload Handling
results.finding: complaint upload writes archive entries to uploads/complaints/ + fileName without path containment (routes/fileUpload.ts:42-45) — zip-slip
results.risk: path-traversal write outside the upload dir; XXE/deserialization from content
results.fix: Verify each entry path stays under the upload root and enforce content-type + size limits before parsing, per the File Upload cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Apply strict limits on payload size, field length, array length, and JSON nesting depth to prevent resource exhaustion
results.title: Payload Limits Not Enforced
results.finding: upload size is only checked to solve a challenge (routes/fileUpload.ts:62), not enforced; no global body-size/field/depth limits set
results.risk: oversized or deeply-nested payloads can exhaust memory/CPU
results.fix: Set explicit body-parser size limits and field/array/depth caps, per the Denial of Service cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Encrypt confidential data at rest using approved algorithms (AES-256-GCM or equivalent); apply full-disk or database-level encryption for all production data stores
results.title: Data Not Encrypted At Rest
results.finding: SQLite DB and uploaded files persist in plaintext; no database/disk encryption
results.risk: disk or backup access exposes user PII and credentials directly
results.fix: Enable database-/disk-level AES-256 encryption for production stores, as the requirement demands.

results.requirement_text: Security tokens, API keys, and cryptographic keys MUST be generated using a cryptographically secure random source and be at least 256 bits (32 bytes) of entropy
results.title: Weak / Hardcoded Keys
results.finding: 24-char hardcoded HMAC secret (lib/insecurity.ts:44) and an embedded 1024-bit RSA private key (lib/insecurity.ts:23)
results.risk: keys are public in source and under-strength — tokens are forgeable
results.fix: Generate >=256-bit keys from a CSPRNG at deploy time and load from a secret manager, never from source.

results.requirement_text: Transmit sensitive data in the HTTP request body (POST/PUT) rather than in URLs or query parameters
results.title: Some Sensitive Data On GET
results.finding: login/password ops use POST, but several sensitive lookups/state reads flow through GET query strings
results.risk: sensitive values captured in logs, proxies, and browser history
results.fix: Move the remaining sensitive parameters into POST/PUT request bodies.

results.requirement_text: Do not store user credentials (passwords); delegate authentication to a central identity service
results.title: User Passwords Stored (MD5)
results.finding: credentials stored locally, hashed with MD5 at lib/insecurity.ts:43
results.risk: trivially crackable hashes on database compromise
results.fix: Delegate authentication to a central identity service rather than storing passwords, as the requirement directs.

results.requirement_text: Secrets (passwords, API keys, certificates) MUST be stored in a dedicated secret manager; rotate them regularly and on suspected compromise
results.title: Secrets Not In A Secret Manager
results.finding: RSA private key and HMAC secret committed in lib/insecurity.ts; no managed storage or rotation
results.risk: credential compromise via repo read; a leaked key stays valid indefinitely
results.fix: Move secrets to a dedicated secret manager with rotation, per the Secrets Management cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: All data transmitted over external networks MUST be encrypted with TLS 1.2 or higher; prefer TLS 1.3 and mTLS for service-to-service communication
results.title: TLS In Transit
results.finding: app serves plain HTTP; external TLS is expected to terminate at a load balancer not in this repo
results.fix: Ensure all external traffic is TLS-encrypted at the edge.

results.requirement_text: Do not disable TLS certificate verification; never set insecure flags such as verify=false, rejectUnauthorized=false, or equivalent
results.title: TLS Verification Not Disabled
results.finding: no rejectUnauthorized/strictSSL or NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED overrides found

results.requirement_text: Integrate automated Software Composition Analysis (SCA) into the CI/CD pipeline; block builds on critical or high vulnerabilities with no fix available
results.title: No SCA In CI
results.finding: CI runs CodeQL (SAST) and ZAP (DAST) but no SCA scan; bom.json generation alone is not scanning
results.risk: known-vulnerable dependencies ship undetected
results.fix: Add an SCA scan (e.g. OWASP Dependency-Check) that blocks the build on critical/high findings.

results.requirement_text: Pin direct dependencies to exact versions and verify integrity (e.g. lockfiles, checksums, or hash pinning); do not use floating version ranges in production builds
results.title: Dependencies Not Pinned / No Lockfile
results.finding: .npmrc sets package-lock=false and no package-lock.json is committed; package.json uses floating ^ ranges
results.risk: non-reproducible builds; unverified, drifting transitive dependencies
results.fix: Commit a lockfile (remove package-lock=false) and pin direct dependencies to exact versions.

results.requirement_text: Pull packages only from approved, internal registries or well-known public registries; do not reference packages from untrusted or personal namespaces
results.title: Approved Registries
results.finding: no evidence of an approved/internal registry pin in .npmrc
results.fix: Pull packages only from approved registries; configure in .npmrc/CI.

results.requirement_text: Generate and publish a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for each production release to enable rapid impact assessment during supply-chain incidents
results.title: SBOM Generated
results.finding: cyclonedx-npm SBOM generation wired in package.json (sbom script)

results.requirement_text: Review transitive dependency trees regularly; remove unused dependencies and replace abandoned packages with maintained alternatives
results.title: Transitive Dependency Review
results.finding: periodic transitive-tree review is a process control not visible in the repo
results.fix: Review transitive trees regularly and remove unused dependencies.

results.requirement_text: Run Static Application Security Testing (SAST) in the CI/CD pipeline on every pull request and fail builds on high-severity findings
results.title: SAST In CI
results.finding: CodeQL SAST workflow at .github/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml

results.requirement_text: Log all security-relevant events (authentication success/failure, authorization failure, privilege escalation, configuration changes) with sufficient context to support incident investigation
results.title: Partial Security-Event Logging
results.finding: winston + morgan access logging exist (lib/logger.ts, server.ts:338) but authn/authz failures and privilege changes are not systematically logged
results.risk: insufficient trail to investigate auth abuse or escalation
results.fix: Emit structured security-event logs for auth success/failure, authz denial, and config changes with request context.

results.requirement_text: Use structured log formats (e.g. JSON) with consistent fields (timestamp, request-id, user-id, action, outcome); avoid free-text-only log entries for security events
results.title: Logs Not Structured
results.finding: winston logger uses format.simple() (free text) at lib/logger.ts:12, not structured JSON
results.risk: security events are hard to parse, correlate, and alert on
results.fix: Switch the winston format to JSON with consistent fields, per the Logging cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Forward security logs to a centralized, tamper-resistant log aggregation system; do not rely solely on local application logs
results.title: Centralized Log Aggregation
results.finding: log forwarding to a central tamper-resistant store is a deployment concern
results.fix: Forward security logs to a centralized, tamper-resistant aggregator.

results.requirement_text: Retain security logs for a minimum of 90 days online and 12 months in cold storage to support forensic investigations and compliance requirements
results.title: Log Retention
results.finding: retention policy is operational, not determinable from code
results.fix: Retain security logs 90d online / 12mo archived per the requirement.

results.requirement_text: Define alerting rules for anomalous patterns (e.g. repeated auth failures, privilege escalation, unusual data access volumes) and route alerts to an on-call channel
results.title: Alerting Rules
results.finding: alerting on anomalous patterns is operational, not in the repo
results.fix: Define alerting rules for repeated auth failures and anomalies.

results.requirement_text: Never include sensitive values (tokens, passwords, PII, credit-card numbers) in log entries at any severity level
results.title: No Sensitive Values In Logs
results.finding: no obvious token/PII logging found, but full log-content review is out of static scope
results.fix: Confirm tokens/PII/PANs are never logged; redact at the logger.

results.requirement_text: Container images MUST be scanned for known vulnerabilities before deployment; block promotion of images with critical or high vulnerabilities that have a fix available
results.title: No Container Image Vulnerability Scan
results.finding: no image scanner (Trivy/Grype) in .github/workflows/; the image is built and shipped unscanned
results.risk: vulnerable OS/runtime layers ship to production undetected
results.fix: Add a Trivy/Grype scan gate to CI that blocks critical/high fixable findings, per the Docker Security cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Containers MUST NOT run as root; set a non-root UID in the Dockerfile and enforce it via a security policy (e.g. PodSecurity admission, OPA)
results.title: Non-Root Container
results.finding: Dockerfile runs as USER 65532 on a distroless base image

results.requirement_text: Mount container filesystems as read-only where possible; use explicitly declared writable volume mounts for required data paths
results.title: Read-Only Container FS
results.finding: read-only root filesystem is a runtime/orchestrator setting not in the repo
results.fix: Mount the container filesystem read-only where possible at runtime.

results.requirement_text: Drop all Linux capabilities not required by the application; do not use privileged containers in production
results.title: Drop Linux Capabilities
results.finding: capability dropping is a runtime/orchestrator setting not in the repo
results.fix: Drop all unneeded Linux capabilities and never add NET_RAW at runtime.

results.requirement_text: Apply network policies (or equivalent micro-segmentation controls) to restrict pod-to-pod communication to explicitly required flows
results.title: Network Policies
results.finding: no Kubernetes/IaC manifests in the repo to which micro-segmentation would apply

results.requirement_text: Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) definitions MUST be scanned with a policy-as-code tool (e.g. tfsec, Checkov) in the CI/CD pipeline; block critical misconfigurations
results.title: IaC Policy Scan
results.finding: repo ships no Terraform/IaC definitions to scan

results.requirement_text: Pin base images to an immutable digest (SHA256) rather than a mutable tag; rebuild images regularly to incorporate upstream security patches
results.title: Base Image Not Pinned To Digest
results.finding: Dockerfile pins by mutable tag (FROM node:24, gcr.io/distroless/nodejs24-debian13), not a SHA256 digest
results.risk: the base image can change under the same tag, undermining reproducibility/provenance
results.fix: Pin each FROM to an immutable @sha256:... digest and rebuild regularly, per the Docker Security cheat sheet.

results.requirement_text: Store IaC templates in version control; apply code review and approval gates before any infrastructure change reaches production
results.title: IaC In Version Control
results.finding: repo ships no production IaC templates

results.requirement_text:
Sanitize and validate all user input before incorporating it into LLM prompts.
Use parameterized prompt templates with strict separation between system instructions and user content.
Never concatenate raw user input directly into system prompts via string formatting.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Filter LLM output before returning it to users to prevent leakage of PII, credentials, internal paths, or training data.
Implement output validation that checks for sensitive patterns (API keys, internal URLs, personal data) before delivery.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Pin LLM SDK and model versions explicitly. Verify model integrity via checksums when loading from external registries.
Use only trusted model sources and maintain an inventory of all AI/ML dependencies.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Validate and sanitize all data ingested into RAG knowledge bases and vector stores.
Restrict who can update the knowledge base and maintain an audit trail for all embedding updates.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Treat all LLM output as untrusted input. Never use LLM output directly in eval(), exec(), raw SQL queries,
shell commands, innerHTML, or downstream API calls without sanitization and validation.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Implement a permission model for LLM tool use. Limit tool access to the minimum required capability.
Require human approval for destructive or irreversible operations (delete, write, execute, send).
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Keep system prompts server-side only; never include them in client-side bundles.
Do not log system prompts at debug level. Do not echo system prompt content in error messages.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Enforce authentication and authorization on all vector database queries.
Rate-limit similarity search endpoints. Validate embedding dimensions and content before indexing.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Clearly label AI-generated content with appropriate disclaimers.
Ground LLM outputs against authoritative data sources where factual accuracy is required.
Log AI-generated responses for audit and correction.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM

results.requirement_text:
Set max_tokens limits on all LLM API calls. Implement per-user and per-session rate limits.
Monitor token consumption and set cost alerting thresholds. Implement circuit breakers for runaway costs.
results.title: LLM Control Not Applicable
results.finding: no real LLM/RAG/vector-DB integration — juicy-chat-bot is a static intent matcher, not an LLM
